title: Maintain your business - Canada.ca
content:
## Maintain your business
It is important to keep your business information complete and up to date with the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). You must notify the CRA about certain changes you make to your business.
- Access your business information online
- Update your business information
- Growing your business
- Changes to business operations

## Access your business information online
To access your business information online, the CRA must have your Social Insurance Number (SIN) on file, and your business number (BN) must be added to your CRA account. Once set up, you can view and manage your program accounts, and authorize representatives using My Business Account in your CRA account .
Only owners, partners, or directors who have their name and SIN on file with the CRA have the authority to:
- Access the business information
- Make changes to the business
- Access My Business Account

### How to add your SIN to your CRA business account
If the SIN of an owner, partner, or director has not been provided to the CRA before, you can add it to your BN in one of the following ways:
An owner, partner, or director can add their SIN online when they add their BN to their CRA account.
To use this service, your business must have a GST/HST account or a corporate income tax account. You must also have a processed GST/HST return or processed corporate income tax return for that account, because you'll be asked to enter information from that return.
For steps on how to add your SIN online, go to How to access My Business Account .
A representative can upload a request using the Submit documents service in the Represent a Client portal of their CRA account .
The request must be signed by the owner, partner, or director asking the CRA to add their SIN to the business account.
An owner, partner, director, or representative can fax a signed request to the business's tax centre:
- Prince Edward Island Tax Centre : 1-833-724-7237
- Sudbury Tax Centre : 1-855-276-1529
An owner, partner, director, or representative can mail a signed request to the business's tax centre.
For addresses, go to Find a CRA address .

## Update your business information
Depending on the type of change, you may be able to update the information in to your CRA account or you may have to contact the CRA.
- Authorize or cancel a representative
- Change of owners, partners, or directors
- Change a business address
- Change a business operating name
- Access to corporate tax information
- Change of legal status
- Change of fiscal year-end
- Change of business activity

## Growing your business
If your business expands, there may be new reporting requirements and you may have to provide new information to the CRA.
- Amalgamation (corporations)
- Opening more locations, branches, or divisions
- Bringing personal assets into your business

## Changes to business operations
If there are changes to your business operations, you must notify the CRA to avoid tax implications that may affect your program accounts.
- Temporarily stopping business operations
- Closing CRA program accounts
- Bankruptcy/receivership
- Retirement or death of business owner or partner

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
